summary: Switch src/main.rs's lone hand-rolled test tempdir to the tempfile-backed tmpdir(label) pattern every other in-src test module already carries

edit.description: `mod tests` (line 1128) has one test, `repo_file_set_stays_raw_disk_including_gitignored_targets` (line 1140), that hand-rolls its temp dir: `let root = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("temper-backing-{}", std::process::id()));` (line 1141) plus a pre-emptive `fs::remove_dir_all(&root)` (line 1142) and a trailing `fs::remove_dir_all(&root)` (line 1157). Every other in-src `#[cfg(test)]` module already carries an identical private helper for this (e.g. `src/frontmatter.rs:565`, `src/check.rs:344`, `src/bundle.rs:329`): `fn tmpdir(label: &str) -> PathBuf { tempfile::Builder::new().prefix(label).tempdir().expect("failed to create temp dir").keep() }`. Add that same helper to `main.rs`'s `mod tests`, replace line 1141 with `let root = tmpdir("repo-file-set");`, and drop both `remove_dir_all` calls (lines 1142 and 1157) to match the sibling modules' convention (no manual cleanup — `tempfile` owns uniqueness, `.keep()` is deliberate per the sibling copies).

tests.asserts: repo_file_set_stays_raw_disk_including_gitignored_targets still passes unchanged — same gitignored-backing-target assertions, now against a tempfile-backed dir

acceptance: main.rs's test module uses the same tmpdir(label) helper every sibling in-src test module carries; no hand-rolled env::temp_dir()+process::id()+manual remove_dir_all construction remains in src/; cargo test green.
notes: Drained from .flume/refactor/build-main-backing-tmpdir.md (observed at 96e947d, re-verified live at HEAD — src/main.rs:1141 unchanged since). Found while build verified TEST-SCAFFOLDING-CONSOLIDATE's acceptance bar: main.rs wasn't in that entry's files.edit (its one-off didn't match the counter+pid+label description), so this straggler is its own entry. Disjoint from every other open/blocked entry (none touch src/main.rs). scoped at 33c1f7f

Rename the function to `tmpdir` and update its 8 call sites (lines 1111, 1141, 1168, 1219, 1234, 1252, 1264, 1276, all `surface_tmpdir("...")`) to `tmpdir("...")`.

Byte-identical body (tempfile::Builder prefix+tempdir+keep) and doc comment to every sibling copy, just a different symbol name — same job, two names. Disjoint from TMPDIR-HELPER-CONSOLIDATE(mainrs) (main.rs only) and every other open entry. scoped at 3a2bed1

summary: Consolidate the byte-identical closing-`---`-delimiter scan loop duplicated in frontmatter.rs's split_frontmatter and install.rs's frontmatter_inner into one home

edit.description:
`split_frontmatter` (line 409) inlines the closing-delimiter scan (lines 417-426: `for line in rest.split_inclusive('
') { let content = line.strip_suffix('
')...; if content.trim_end() == "---" { ... } offset += line.len(); }`) after its own opening-delimiter check. Extract the scan into `pub(crate) fn closing_delimiter(rest: &str) -> Option<(&str, &str)>` returning `(matter, body)` slices of `rest`; `split_frontmatter` keeps its opening check (`raw.split_once('
')`, `first.trim_end() != "---"`) and delegates the rest to it.

Delete `frontmatter_inner` (line 1463) — its own doc comment admits it 'mirrors the delimiter detection the loaders use': a byte-for-byte copy of the same scan loop, returning only the matter half. Its three call sites — `project_modeline` (line 1412), `project_note` (line 1441), `placement_lines` (line 1481), each already holding `rest = source.strip_prefix("---\n")?` — use `crate::frontmatter::closing_delimiter(rest).map(|(matter, _)| matter)`.

tests.asserts: modeline-insertion and managed-by-note projection (project_modeline/project_note/placement_lines-driven discovery-report behavior) is unchanged for frontmatter with and without a closing delimiter
the in-source frontmatter split tests (with/without a closing `---`, with a false-positive `---`-looking body line) are unchanged

acceptance: One closing-delimiter scanner (`crate::frontmatter::closing_delimiter`, `pub(crate)`); no duplicate scan loop remains in install.rs; `cargo test` green.
notes: Job 4 residue sweep at 425968d: both functions' doc comments admit they 'mirror' the same scan. gray_matter unused here (pinned-semantics exception). Blocker TEST-SCAFFOLDING-CONSOLIDATE shipped at 1894af9; unblocked to open. Re-verified at HEAD: touches only frontmatter.rs/install.rs, untouched by that ship — cited lines unchanged (split_frontmatter 409, frontmatter_inner 1463, project_modeline 1412, project_note 1441, placement_lines 1481). Disjoint from other open entries. scoped at 95689ae

summary: Consolidate 2 inline `\`->`/` path-separator-normalize reimplementations (document.rs, coverage_note.rs) into drift.rs's existing to_lock_path helper

edit.description: `fn to_lock_path(path: &Path) -> String` (line 318) is `path.to_string_lossy().replace('\\', "/")` — the one named home for lock-row path normalization, currently private and called only within this file (lines 487, 501). Make it `pub(crate)` so document.rs and coverage_note.rs (below) can call it instead of reimplementing the same mechanic inline.

`add_provenance` (line 323) writes `source_path.replace('\\', "/")` inline (line 327) on its `source_path: &str` parameter, duplicating drift.rs's `to_lock_path` mechanic byte-for-byte. Add `use crate::drift;` and replace line 327's value with `Value::from(drift::to_lock_path(Path::new(source_path)))`.

The ignore-aware raw-disk-walk closure (already `use crate::drift;` at line 25) does `rel.to_string_lossy().replace('\\', "/")` inline at line 196 on `rel: &Path` (from `entry.path().strip_prefix(root)`), duplicating the same mechanic. Replace with `drift::to_lock_path(rel)`.

tests.asserts: to_lock_path_normalizes_a_backslash_joined_path still passes unchanged now that the fn is pub(crate) and called cross-module
add_provenance's in-source lock-module round-trip tests are unchanged
the raw-disk-walk tests (unmodeled-surface suppression fixtures) are unchanged, including on a path containing no backslashes

acceptance: document.rs and coverage_note.rs's inline `\`->`/` normalization is replaced by calls to the now-`pub(crate)` `drift::to_lock_path`; no hand-rolled `.replace('\\', "/")` remains at those two sites; cargo test green.
notes: Job 4 residue sweep at b749f80: 5 sites reimplement this mechanic (drift.rs's home plus 4 inline copies); install.rs even cites a `(PATH-SEP-NORMALIZE)` tag as if a shared convention existed. Scoped to the 2 sites disjoint from all open entries — main.rs:918 and install.rs:676/678 carry the identical duplicate but share files with open TMPDIR-HELPER-CONSOLIDATE(mainrs)/FRONTMATTER-DELIMITER-SCAN-CONSOLIDATE, deferred per disjoint-or-serialize. scoped at b749f80

summary: Consolidate 7 inline lexicographic string-comparator reimplementations in sdk/src/declarations.ts and emit.ts into one compareStrings helper

edit.description: The stable-sort comparator `(a, b) => (a < b ? -1 : a > b ? 1 : 0)` is reimplemented inline 6 times over different key projections: `templatesFor` (line 222, plain strings), `atLocusKindsInPlay` (line 260, `.name`), `requirementRows` (line 285, destructured tuple key), `satisfiesRows` (lines 319-323, two-key `.member`/`.requirement` chained with `||`), `mentionRows` (lines 341-345, two-key `.member`/`.target`), `compileDeclarations`'s clause-binding sort (line 353, `.kind.key`). Add one `export function compareStrings(a: string, b: string): number { return a < b ? -1 : a > b ? 1 : 0; }` and rewrite each of the 6 sites to call it (chained two-key sites become `compareStrings(a.x, b.x) || compareStrings(a.y, b.y)`).

`orderedMembers` (lines 237-240) reimplements the same two-key chained comparator (`.kind` then `.name`) a 7th time. Import `compareStrings` alongside the existing `compileDeclarations, encodeSeam` import from `./declarations.js` (line 20) and rewrite the sort to `compareStrings(a.kind, b.kind) || compareStrings(a.name, b.name)`.

tests.asserts: compileDeclarations's six declaration families (kinds, clauses, requirements, assembly facts, satisfies, mentions) and the emitted payload's member ordering are unchanged for every existing fixture harness

acceptance: sdk/src/declarations.ts exports one compareStrings; no inline `a < b ? -1 : a > b ? 1 : 0` comparator remains in declarations.ts or emit.ts; `pnpm --dir sdk test` green.
notes: Job 4 residue sweep at HEAD (b749f80): same mechanic (a lexicographic string comparator), reimplemented per callsite instead of factored into one named function — the SDK-side instance of 'two matchers, two normalizers, two encoders' residue. Disjoint from every other open entry (none touch sdk/). SDK is deliberately zero-dependency so this is an in-language consolidation, not a libraries-before-hand-rolls case. scoped at b749f80

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, now at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
